strengths: Clear identification of a real client with interview transcripts
Partial product rationale explaining user confidence and utility
weaknesses: No formal successâcriteria table or structured evaluation metrics
Client investigation lacks depth on technical constraints and broader context
Planning section is not clearly delineated before appendices
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission demonstrates an appropriate scenario and client consultation (Band 3â4) and a partially explained product rationale (Band 3â4), but the success criteria are only implied (Band 1â2). Overall, the planning evidence falls in the middle of the rubric, warranting 3 of 6 marks.

strengths: Appropriate use of Java Swing, JDBC and MySQL
Moderate complexity: multiple windows, image handling, CRUD operations
Code listings include comments and a bibliography identifies sources
weaknesses: Ingenuity is moderate rather than high-level (no advanced algorithms)
Limited explanation of database normalization and schema rationale
GUI components are not encapsulated, affecting maintainability
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands fall into the 5â8 band: complexity and ingenuity are moderate, tools are used effectively, and sources are identified with explanations. An overall score of 5 out of 12 reflects solid but not outstanding development work.

strengths: Includes a basic textual design overview of the GUI windows
Contains some screenshots illustrating key UI components
weaknesses: No development log or record of tasks
No systematic test plan or test-case table
Design overview is limited and lacks diagrams or process mapping
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: With only a minimal design overview (Band 1â2, 2 marks) and both the record of tasks and test plan missing (Band 0), the overall Solution Overview reflects limited evidence of the development process and warrants 2 of 6 marks.

strengths: Product functions reliably with CRUD operations and image display
UI flow demonstrated clearly in screenshots
weaknesses: Extensibility is not documented or architecturally justified
Credentials are hardâcoded, reducing ease of configuration
Edge cases (e.g., empty searches or error handling) are not shown
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The product functions well (Band 3â4) but the extensibility element is only moderately straightforward (Band 3â4 at the lower end). Consequently, 3 of 4 marks appropriately reflect Functionality and Extensibility.

strengths: Comprehensive client/adviser feedback with concrete suggestions
Recommendations (categorisation, multilingual support, predictive search) are realistic
weaknesses: No structured evaluation mapping outcomes to success criteria
Limited analysis of how well each criterion was met
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While client feedback is strong (Band 5â6) and recommendations are realistic (Band 3â4), evaluation against success criteria is only a limited attempt (Band 1â2). The balance of these strands justifies 3 of 6 marks.

strandSelections.reasoning: **Design Overview** â Apart from extensive code listings and screenshots, there is only a brief textual outline of windows.  There are no diagrams (flowcharts, ERD, class diagrams) and little indication of how components relate.  This is a limited overview that âpoorly demonstratesâ product development, so band 1-2 with the upper value (2) is justified.

**Product Rationale** â The student explains that the program will allow the client to look up emoji meanings easily and feel more confident in group chats; this shows a partial explanation of why the chosen product is suitable.  There is no comparative analysis of alternative solutions or discussion of feasibility, so the rationale is not âfully justifiedâ.  Mid-band (3) in 3-4 is appropriate.

**Use of Tools** â Java Swing, JDBC, MySQL and SQL scripting are used appropriately to build the solution; GUI builders are not relied upon, and SQL scripts are executed programmatically.  Usage is appropriate and effective but not especially advanced, so the bottom of band 5-8 (5) fits best.
**Test Plan** â No test-plan table or systematic outline of tests is provided.  Therefore this strand receives band 0.

**Future Recommendations** â The student records the clientâs suggestions (categorisation, multilingual support, predictive search) but provides minimal justification or prioritisation.  Recommendations are largely realistic yet only partially justified, meriting the mid-band (3-4) with a score of 3.

**Documentation & Sources** â The appendix contains code listings with comments and a bibliography that identifies external tutorials and resources.  Explanations of major techniques (JDBC connection, prepared statements, normalisation) are brief but present.  This satisfies âall sources identifiedâ with some explanation, matching the lower end of band 5-8.
**Record of Tasks** â The submission provides no development log, timeline, or table of tasks.  Consequently, band 0 is required.

**Complexity & Ingenuity** â The product employs a GUI with multiple windows, dynamic population from a MySQL database, CRUD operations, image handling and input normalisation.  These elements represent a moderate level of algorithmic complexity and inventiveness but not an advanced algorithmic challenge.  The lower end of band 5-8 (score 5) is appropriate.

**Success Criteria** â No formal, itemised success-criteria table is included; any criteria are implied only in narrative form (e.g., âsimple to useâ, âshows meaningâ).  This is generally inappropriate for evaluation purposes, so the strand falls in the lowest non-zero band (1-2) and merits the minimum mark of 1.

**Client / Adviser Feedback** â The appendix includes detailed feedback from the client at both prototype and final stages, with concrete suggestions (category browsing, multilingual support, predictive search).  This is comprehensive, so band 5-6 with the lower score (5) is appropriate.

**Extensibility** â Because data are stored in a relational table and GUI elements reuse helper methods, adding new emoji or extra fields would be straightforward.  No architectural commentary is given, so the strand earns band 3-4 but at the lower mark (3).

**Scenario & Client Investigation** â The submission clearly identifies a real client (the studentâs mother) and provides two interview transcripts that show the clientâs needs and reactions at both prototype and final stages.  However, the discussion stays conversational and lacks depth on technical constraints or broader context of use, so the evidence of consultation is present but not âdetailedâ or âthoroughâ.  Band 3-4 with the upper value (4) best reflects this quality.

**Product Functionality** â Screenshots and code indicate that list, search, add and delete operations work, and the results window displays images.  No critical flaws are evident, so âproduct functions wellâ applies; award the top of band 3-4 (4).

**Evaluation vs. Success Criteria** â The report lacks a structured evaluation section; any reflection is implicit in the client conversation.  This constitutes only a limited attempt, so award the top of band 1-2 (2).

annotations.comment: Delete result screenshot includes success message but does not capture the case when no record is found. Include that scenario to validate error handling.
No UML class diagram or ERD accompanies this class declaration. Adding diagrams would clarify architecture and component interactions in the design overview.
List window screenshot shows the UI but does not demonstrate handling of lengthy lists or scroll bars. Include a test case and screenshot for list overflow or pagination.
The search window screenshot illustrates basic functionality, but there is no evidence of dynamic search results or invalid-input handling. Add a screenshot of the error message scenario.
Result window screenshot shows detail display, demonstrating successful retrieval and image rendering with real data.
Delete window screenshot clearly shows the delete UI, but adding a confirmation dialog before deletion would improve usability and prevent accidental removals.
Add result screenshot confirms user feedback on successful addition, demonstrating immediate and clear confirmation.

The main report lacks a dedicated planning section outlining the scenario, client investigation, and structured success criteria. Introduce a Planning section before appendices to present client evidence, product rationale, and formal success criteria.
Imports are properly selected to support GUI and database operations, demonstrating effective use of Java libraries.
The import table lacks justification for key dependencies. Consider explaining in a design overview why each library is necessary for GUI, database, and input normalization.
UI components are all declared individually, which makes maintenance harder. Consider encapsulating related controls into panels or custom components for readability and reuse.
The database credentials are hard-coded (url, username, password). Move them to a configuration file or environment variables to improve security and facilitate deployment.
Although the SQL dump is included, there is no accompanying test plan to validate the database setup and queries. Add a Test Plan section listing test cases and expected results for CRUD operations.
The SQL schema is presented but lacks commentary on normalization, keys, and table relationships. Include a brief explanation in the design overview to justify your database structure.
The welcome window screenshot clearly demonstrates UI layout and initial functionality, indicating that the product functions as intended for the first interaction.
Screenshot of the main menu lacks annotations explaining button functions. Add captions or callouts to guide evaluators through the UI flow and functionality.
Error window screenshot demonstrates error handling but lacks consistent styling and a link to the corresponding test case. Recommend documenting this in the test plan.
The client interview is presented as continuous dialogue without clearly documented requirements or priorities. Structure key requirements in bullet points for clarity.
Prototype discussion is insightful but lacks visual prototypes or wireframes linked in the report. Embed or reference prototype designs for full context.
There is no dedicated evaluation section mapping outcomes against the success criteria. Include a formal evaluation to demonstrate attainment of each criterion.
The bibliography lists only two sources; expand references to include design methodology guides, testing frameworks, and relevant API documentation.
Appendix sections are extensive, but the report lacks a concise solution overview summarising key tasks, design steps, and test plan. Add that overview before the code listings.
The appendix headings are clear, but there is no high-level record of tasks or project timeline. Include a development log to demonstrate your process.
